skill.name: openai-whisper-api
skill.description: Transcribe audio via OpenAI Audio Transcriptions API (Whisper).
skill.summary: Transcribe audio files to text using OpenAI's Whisper API with curl
skill.author: mylukin

security_audit.summary: All 33 static findings evaluated as false positives. This is a legitimate OpenAI Whisper API integration skill that uses shell scripts to call OpenAI's audio transcription endpoint. The API key access is required for authentication. Network calls go to api.openai.com. Filesystem access is limited to controlled output paths. No user input injection vectors exist in the shell script. No prompt injection attempts detected.

content.user_title: Transcribe audio files to text with Whisper API
content.value_statement: Convert audio files to accurate text transcriptions using OpenAI's Whisper model. Perfect for podcasts, meetings, interviews, and voice recordings.
actual_capabilities: Transcribe audio files to text using OpenAI Whisper model
Support multiple audio formats (m4a, ogg, mp3, wav, and more)
Specify language for improved transcription accuracy
Add prompt hints to improve speaker name and terminology recognition
Output as plain text or JSON format
Use OpenAI-compatible proxies for local or custom endpoints
limitations: Requires valid OpenAI API key with Whisper API access
Audio files must be accessible locally or via mounted paths
Maximum file size limited by OpenAI API (25MB for most configurations)
transcription quality depends on audio clarity and background noise

use_cases.title: Meeting and Interview Transcription
use_cases.description: Automatically convert recorded meetings, interviews, or conference calls to searchable text for documentation and reference.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers, journalists, and professionals who conduct interviews or meetings

use_cases.title: Podcast Episode Text Conversion
use_cases.description: Generate accurate text transcripts of podcast episodes for show notes, accessibility features, or content repurposing.
use_cases.target_user: Podcasters and content creators who want to maximize content reach

use_cases.title: Voice Recording Documentation
use_cases.description: Transcribe voice memos, recorded notes, or dictated content into text for easy editing and distribution.
use_cases.target_user: Writers, note-takers, and anyone who records voice notes

prompt_templates.title: Basic Audio Transcription
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the openai-whisper-api skill to transcribe the audio file at /path/to/recording.m4a to text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick transcription of a single audio file

prompt_templates.title: Transcription with Language Hint
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe /path/to/meeting.mp3 using the Whisper skill. The audio is in English so use --language en for better accuracy.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you know the language of the audio

prompt_templates.title: Transcription with Speaker Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the openai-whisper-api skill to transcribe /path/to/interview.m4a. Add a prompt with the speaker names: Sarah, Michael, and David.
prompt_templates.scenario: For interviews where you know the participants

prompt_templates.title: Batch Transcription with JSON Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these audio files to JSON transcripts using the Whisper skill: file1.m4a, file2.ogg, file3.wav. Use --json flag for structured output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple files for programmatic analysis

output_examples.output:
Here's the transcription from the meeting recording:

[00:00] Welcome everyone. Let's start with the quarterly review.
[00:15] Sarah: I'd like to begin by discussing the revenue numbers.
[00:32] Michael: The Q3 results show a 15% increase compared to last year.
[01:05] Sarah: That's excellent news. What about the regional breakdown?
...
{"text": "Remember to follow up with the team about the project deadline. We need to finalize the design by Friday.", "language": "en", "duration": 12.5}
Using prompt hints for speaker names results in more accurate transcription: "Peter: I'll send the updated proposal tomorrow. Daniel: Great, I'll review it first thing Monday morning."

best_practices: Use high-quality audio recordings for best transcription accuracy
Set the --language flag when the audio language is known to improve results
Provide speaker names and key terminology via --prompt for domain-specific content
anti_patterns: Do not transcribe extremely long audio files in a single request; split them into segments
Avoid using low bitrate or heavily compressed audio formats that degrade quality
Do not skip the language specification for non-English audio when the language is known

faq.question: What audio formats are supported by the Whisper API?
faq.answer: The OpenAI Whisper API supports many formats including m4a, mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, wav, webm, and ogg. The skill passes these directly to the API.

faq.question: How do I set up my OpenAI API key?
faq.answer: Set the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable with your key from platform.openai.com. You can also configure it in your OpenClaw config file under the openai-whisper-api skill settings.

faq.question: Can I use a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes, set the OPENAI_BASE_URL environment variable to your custom endpoint. The skill documentation shows how to configure local proxies like LocalAI or Ollama.

faq.question: How accurate is the Whisper transcription?
faq.answer: Whisper provides highly accurate transcriptions, typically better than 85% accuracy for clear English audio. Accuracy varies based on audio quality, background noise, and accent clarity.

faq.question: What is the maximum audio file size?
faq.answer: OpenAI's Whisper API has a 25MB default limit. For longer recordings, split the audio into multiple segments before transcription.

faq.question: Can I transcribe non-English audio?
faq.answer: Yes, Whisper supports many languages. Use the --language flag to specify the language code (en, es, fr, de, etc.) for improved accuracy.
